what_it_does: Acceptance harness for three GPU STT ears (parakeet, canary, granite). For each ear service that is reachable, it synthesizes a known audio clip via a live Kokoro TTS endpoint, posts it to the ear's /inference endpoint through voice.stt.transcribe, asserts a non-empty transcript containing expected keywords, and records the nvidia-smi VRAM delta around the load. If an ear is down or the TTS service is unreachable, it skips loudly and ultimately exits non-zero.
observations: Uses a live Kokoro TTS service (default http://127.0.0.1:4123/tts) to generate the test audio fixture.
Probes each ear's liveness with an HTTP GET to the provider's root URL and a 200-499 status check.
Measures GPU VRAM before and after each transcription via nvidia-smi, preferring /usr/lib/wsl/lib/nvidia-smi when present.
Implements an explicit fail-loud skip policy: down services are reported and counted as failures, never as vacuous passes.
Exits 1 if any ear is skipped or any assertion fails, and also if no ear service is up at all.
Imports voice.stt to obtain the provider registry and route transcription calls.
Hardcodes the ear list ["parakeet","canary","granite"] and relies on voice_stt.STT_PROVIDERS for URLs.
Maintains global mutable state (PASS counter and ok flag) across helper functions.
standouts: Fail-loud skip design: unavailable downstream services produce a non-zero exit rather than a neutral skip.
VRAM measurement is treated as the ground-truth replacement for estimated model memory footprints.
The test fixture is generated live by an external TTS service rather than using a checked-in audio file.
conventions: Follows an end-to-end acceptance-test pattern of probe, act, assert, and measure.
Breaks pure unit-test isolation by requiring live HTTP services, a GPU, and nvidia-smi.
Uses global mutable state (PASS, ok) mutated by helper functions.
Mixes a long explanatory module docstring and inline comments with executable code.
concerns: Global PASS/ok state mutated by check() makes the control flow harder to trace.
ear_up() only checks reachability of the root URL, not the actual /inference endpoint or model readiness.
kokoro_clip() silently swallows exceptions and returns None, losing failure details.
vram_used_mib() assumes the first GPU and a simple nvidia-smi output format without validation.
The provider list is hardcoded and could drift from the registry.
notes: A live-service acceptance harness that proves GPU STT ears by actual use, measures their VRAM cost, and refuses to report success when services are unavailable.
questions: What exactly does voice_stt.available_stt() return and how are STT_PROVIDERS populated?
What is the precise /inference request and response contract for each ear?
How is the Kokoro TTS service expected to be started, and what audio format does it return?
What does 'needs_tim' refer to in the broader project context?
Are the transcript keyword expectations shared across all ears or tuned per model?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is exactly the transcribe-plus-measured-VRAM acceptance harness with loud skips that the docstring describes.
intention_signals: To provide a trustworthy, environment-conditioned acceptance gate for GPU speech-to-text services that records real VRAM cost only when services are actually running, while clearly distinguishing code readiness from deployment-blocked states.
novelty: The explicit 'skip loud' policy that treats unavailable downstream services as a non-zero test outcome rather than a neutral skip, and the use of live TTS synthesis as the test fixture.
contribution: A runnable, environment-aware acceptance script that verifies GPU STT transcription accuracy against a synthesized clip and captures measured VRAM deltas, with clear reporting of service availability and skip/failure states.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for three GPU STT ear services that synthesizes a known clip via Kokoro TTS, posts it to each running ear's /inference endpoint, asserts transcript content, records nvidia-smi VRAM deltas, and fails loudly when services are unavailable.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a live-service, measured-VRAM acceptance gate for GPU speech-to-text ears that refuses vacuous passes when services are down.

symbols.does: Increments PASS and prints PASS if cond is truthy; otherwise marks the global ok flag False and prints FAIL.
symbols.description: Central assertion helper that maintains a global pass count and overall success flag, printing per-check results and contributing to the final exit code.

symbols.does: Queries nvidia-smi for the first GPU's used memory in MiB, preferring the WSL path when present.
symbols.description: Wraps an nvidia-smi subprocess call to capture current GPU memory usage; used to compute per-ear VRAM deltas around transcription loads.

symbols.does: Checks whether a registered STT provider's root URL responds with an HTTP status in the 200-499 range within 3 seconds.
symbols.description: Simple liveness probe that determines whether a GPU ear service is reachable before attempting transcription; a false result triggers a loud skip.

symbols.does: Synthesizes audio bytes by POSTing text to the Kokoro TTS endpoint configured via COMPANY_TTS_URL.
symbols.description: Generates the test audio fixture on demand from a live TTS service; returns None silently if the request fails, leaving transcription to be skipped.
